target.title: fix(slack): bridge presentation capabilities and renderPresentation through channel outbound facade
target.body:
## Summary

Two bugs caused Slack presentation payloads (Block Kit) to render as plain text instead of cards:

1. **Missing facade bridge** (`extensions/slack/src/channel.ts`): The `slackChannelOutbound` facade did not expose `presentationCapabilities` or `renderPresentation`. The core delivery pipeline calls `renderPresentationForDelivery`, which checks `handler.renderPresentation` from the channel outbound adapter. Since it was `undefined`, the pipeline fell back to `renderMessagePresentationFallbackText`, converting the presentation to plain text before send. The delegated `slackOutbound` adapter already had a working `renderPresentation` and full `presentationCapabilities` (including limits), but the facade never forwarded them.

2. **Incorrect receipt kind priority** (`src/channels/message/outbound-bridge.ts`): `resolvePayloadReceiptKind` checked `text` before `presentation`, so messages with both text and presentation classified as `"text"` instead of `"card"`.

Fixes #95440

## Changes

- **`extensions/slack/src/channel.ts`**: Bridge the full `presentationCapabilities` (including `limits` for actions, selects, and text) and `renderPresentation` from `slackChannelOutbound` to the existing `slackOutbound` implementation.
- **`src/channels/message/outbound-bridge.ts`**: Move the `presentation`/`interactive` check above the `text` check in `resolvePayloadReceiptKind`.
- **`src/channels/message/outbound-bridge.test.ts`**: Add regression test for text+presentation → kind `"card"`.

## Change Type

- [x] Bug fix

## Scope

- [ ] Gateway / [ ] Skills / [ ] Auth / [ ] Memory / [x] Integrations / [x] API / [ ] UI/UX / [ ] CI

## Real behavior proof

**Behavior addressed:** Slack presentation payload (Block Kit) is delivered as plain text (`kind: "text"`) instead of card (`kind: "card"`). The root cause is the missing `presentationCapabilities` and `renderPresentation` bridge on `slackChannelOutbound`, plus incorrect receipt kind priority.

**Environment tested:** Node.js v24.13.1 on Linux, OpenClaw main branch and PR branch.

**Exact steps run after the patch:** Called `createChannelMessageAdapterFromOutbound` with `sendPayload` handler for receipt kind testing. Called `slackPlugin.outbound.presentationCapabilities` and `slackPlugin.outbound.renderPresentation` directly to verify the Slack facade bridge through the real production code path.

**Evidence before fix (upstream/main — bug present):**

```text
=== outbound-bridge receipt kind ===
text + presentation → text (expected: card) ❌
presentation only  → card (expected: card) ✅
text only           → text (expected: text) ✅

=== Slack facade presentation bridge ===
presentationCapabilities.supported: undefined (expected: true) ❌
presentationCapabilities.limits defined: false (expected: true) ❌
renderPresentation defined: false (expected: true) ❌
renderPresentation produces blocks: n/a ❌
```

**Evidence after fix (PR branch — bug fixed):**

```text
=== outbound-bridge receipt kind ===
text + presentation → card (expected: card) ✅
presentation only  → card (expected: card) ✅
text only           → text (expected: text) ✅

=== Slack facade presentation bridge ===
presentationCapabilities.supported: true (expected: true) ✅
presentationCapabilities.buttons: true (expected: true) ✅
presentationCapabilities.divider: true (expected: true) ✅
presentationCapabilities.limits defined: true (expected: true) ✅
  limits.actions.maxActionsPerRow: 25 (expected: 25) ✅
  limits.selects.maxOptions: 100 (expected: 100) ✅
  limits.text.maxLength: 8000 (expected: SLACK_TEXT_LIMIT) ✅
renderPresentation defined: true (expected: true) ✅
renderPresentation produces blocks: true (expected: true) ✅
```

**Observed result after the fix:** Receipt kind correctly resolves as `"card"` for presentation payloads even when text is also present. The Slack facade now exposes `presentationCapabilities` with full limits and `renderPresentation` that delegates to `slackOutbound.renderPresentation`, producing valid Slack Block Kit blocks. Text-only and presentation-only paths are unchanged.

**What was not tested:** Live Slack delivery with `openclaw message send --presentation` against a real Slack workspace.
target.author: zoowh
